fields.description:
# Wire run_eval (Inspect Task) + eval-live CI (2.5)

## What

Replace the `run_eval()` NotImplementedError with a thin Inspect AI Task assembly:
dataset -> solver (task 2.2) -> generator call -> scorers (registry, task 2.1) ->
epochs/at_least(k)/coverage/judge_alignment(kappa over gold_set) -> JUnit via to_junit.
Behind the `eval` extra (guard_import). Then wire `.github/workflows/eval.yml`
`eval-live` to actually run for all specs, with community-standard CI + cost rails.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `run_eval()` runs a real Inspect eval for any spec (no longer raises NotImplementedError); offline discipline functions remain the pinned logic.
- [ ] Deterministic scorers gate; llm-judge reports; coverage + kappa applied; JUnit emitted.
- [ ] `eval-live` triggers on workflow_dispatch + weekly schedule (job `if:` includes 'schedule'), `continue-on-error: true` (non-blocking), and `concurrency: cancel-in-progress`. Cost rails: cases run SEQUENTIALLY (run_eval has no model fan-out; concurrency 1 under the workflow cap), a per-run case ceiling via `REBAR_EVAL_MAX_CASES` (passed to `run_eval(max_cases=...)`), and `actions/cache` on the pip cache (the native loop has no promptfoo response-cache dir to cache).
- [ ] Fork-safe: live calls never run on fork PRs/Dependabot; no pull_request_target checking out fork code; spend-cap guidance documented.
- [ ] `eval-discipline` remains the only required PR check (zero-cost).
